PA Skills — Help

Bank of Baroda CSV Data Analyzer Convert Credit Card — Sort PDFs Credit Card — Transactions Document Summarizer GnuCash Import HDFC HSBC ICICI ITR Workbook Import Statement Intercompany Matrix Intercompany Reco Journal KRChoksey Kotak MSG / Email Parser Parser Generator Reconcile Text Translator

User Guide

Every skill writes to the Data/outputs/ folder with a timestamped filename. Hover the ℹ️ icons for input tips and how to read each output.

Bank of Baroda offline · no LLM direct

Turns one or more Bank of Baroda transaction-statement PDFs into a single flat CSV — one row per transaction. Runs fully offline (no LLM, no internet) and correctly stitches tables that overflow across pages without repeating the column header, so a transaction that wraps from the bottom of one page to the top of the next is captured as a single clean row.

When to use it. Use this when you have BoB account statements as PDFs and want them as a tidy CSV you can open in Excel, reconcile, or feed into the GnuCash Import pipeline (choose "Bank of Baroda" there to do extraction + account mapping in one step instead). This skill is extraction only — it does not categorise or assign accounts.

Inputs

  • Bank of Baroda statement PDF(s) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: PDF (.pdf) — one or many, in any order.

How to run

  1. Click the input and pick one or more Bank of Baroda statement PDFs.
  2. Click Run — it processes offline in a few seconds.
  3. Download the CSV, or open it from the outputs folder (see below).

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-BoB.csv
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-BoB.csvℹ️ — One row per transaction with columns: Date, Particulars, Cheque No., Withdrawals, Deposits, Balance. Rows are in statement order; multi-page transactions are merged so no row is split.

Tips

The running balance carries straight from the statement — if the final Balance matches your passbook, the extraction tied out. Re-run with more PDFs to append additional periods (each run writes a new timestamped file). Columns are kept verbatim from the statement so amounts are not rounded or reformatted.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
The output CSV is empty or has very few rows.The PDF is probably a scanned image, not a text PDF. Re-download the original statement from BoB net-banking, or run it through an OCR step first. This skill does not OCR.
Run fails saying the PDF could not be opened.The PDF is password-protected. Unlock it (open in a viewer, print/save as a new unprotected PDF) and try again.
Closing balance in the CSV does not match my passbook.Check you included every page/period and that no statement was selected twice. Each run lists rows in statement order so you can scan for a gap.

CSV Data Analyzer needs LLM endpoint agent

Ask a plain-English question about any CSV and get a written answer with the numbers worked out. The agent loads the file, reasons over the rows, and returns a short Markdown report.

When to use it. Use this for quick, ad-hoc analysis ("average revenue by region?", "which month had the most refunds?") without writing spreadsheet formulas. For a formatted spreadsheet deliverable, use Excel directly instead.

Inputs

  • CSV file (required) ℹ️
    accepts: CSV (.csv) with a header row.
  • Question about this data (e.g. 'What is the average revenue by region?') (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Free text.

How to run

  1. Pick your CSV file.
  2. Type your question.
  3. Click Run and read (or download) the Markdown answer.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-analysis.md
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-analysis.mdℹ️ — A Markdown write-up answering your question, including the figures the agent computed.

Tips

Treat the answer as a first pass — spot-check any number that drives a decision, since the model reasons over the data rather than running fixed formulas.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model.This skill needs an LLM. Start Ollama or configure an endpoint in Settings.
Answer seems to ignore part of the data.Large CSVs may be sampled; narrow the file or the question and re-run.

Convert offline · no LLM direct

Converts a Form 26AS PDF (the TRACES Annual Tax Statement) into a structured Excel workbook with one sheet per Part (Part I through Part X) and inline per-deductor sub-totals, so TDS, TCS, advance tax, self-assessment tax and refunds are each laid out as a clean, sortable table. Runs fully offline.

When to use it. Use this when you have your 26AS PDF from the TRACES portal and want it as Excel to review or reconcile against your ITR. To then post the TDS into GnuCash as journal entries, run "26AS Journal" on the workbook this produces. Do not use it for AIS or TIS — those are different documents.

Inputs

  • Form 26AS PDF (required) ℹ️
    accepts: PDF (.pdf) — a single TRACES-issued 26AS.

How to run

  1. Pick your 26AS PDF.
  2. Click Run — it processes offline in a few seconds.
  3. Open the Excel workbook and use the per-Part sheets.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-26AS.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-26AS.xlsxℹ️ — One worksheet per Part (I–X). Each transaction is a flat row; each deductor block ends with a sub-total row so you can tie figures back to the source statement without re-adding them.

Tips

Amounts are kept verbatim from the statement (no rounding). Cross-check the Part I TDS total against the TDS you are claiming in your return.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Sheets are empty or the text is garbled.The PDF is a scan. Re-download the text 26AS from TRACES; this skill does not OCR.
Columns look wrong / parts are missing.The file is likely an AIS or TIS, not a 26AS. Use the correct 26AS PDF.

Credit Card — Sort PDFs offline · no LLM direct

Takes a messy folder of credit-card statements and organises it: it pulls PDF attachments out of Outlook .msg emails, decrypts password-protected PDFs (via qpdf), and sorts everything by bank/issuer into a clean folder tree. Runs fully offline.

When to use it. Use this as the first step when you have a pile of statement emails and PDFs from several cards. Once sorted, run "CC Transactions" on the decrypted folder to extract the actual transactions.

Inputs

  • Input folder (MSG files and/or PDFs) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: A folder of .msg and/or .pdf files.
  • PDF password(s), comma-separated (or leave blank for auto-detect) (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: Text — one password, or several comma-separated. Optional.

How to run

  1. Select the folder holding your statement emails / PDFs.
  2. Optionally enter PDF password(s); leave blank to auto-detect.
  3. Click Run, then open the output folder.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-CC-Sort/
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-CC-Sort/ (a folder)ℹ️ — Contains Decrypted_PDFs_Correct/ — the unlocked, de-duplicated PDFs to feed into CC Transactions — alongside copies sorted by issuer. Use the "Open output folder" button to browse it.

Tips

This step never reads transaction amounts; it only unlocks and organises. The folder it produces is the exact input the CC Transactions skill expects.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
A PDF stays locked / is skipped.Enter its password in the password box (comma-separate multiple) and re-run.
Source-mode run fails on decryption.qpdf must be on PATH in source mode; the frozen build bundles it.

Credit Card — Transactions offline · no LLM direct

Reads the organised credit-card PDFs from the CC Sort step and extracts every transaction table into one consolidated Excel workbook — one row per transaction across all cards. Runs fully offline.

When to use it. Use this as the second step, right after "CC Sort". It expects the decrypted, sorted folder that CC Sort produced, not raw statement files.

Inputs

  • Organized PDFs folder (Decrypted_PDFs_Correct/ from CC Sort) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: A folder of decrypted statement PDFs.

How to run

  1. Run CC Sort first if you have not already.
  2. Select the Decrypted_PDFs_Correct/ folder.
  3. Click Run and download the Excel.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-CC-Transactions.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-CC-Transactions.xlsxℹ️ — One consolidated sheet of transactions across all sorted statements, with date, description and amount columns extracted from each card's table.

Tips

Because the PDFs are already grouped by issuer, the consolidated sheet keeps cards together — handy for per-card filtering in Excel.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Few or no transactions extracted.You probably pointed at the wrong folder. Use Decrypted_PDFs_Correct/ from CC Sort.

Document Summarizer needs LLM endpoint direct

Summarises any PDF or text file into a concise Markdown summary using your configured LLM.

When to use it. Use this for a quick readable summary of a document — a report, a long text file, a contract. For asking specific questions of tabular data, use CSV Data Analyzer instead.

Inputs

  • Document (PDF or text file) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: PDF (.pdf) or text (.txt, .md, .csv, .log, .json, .xml, .html) — one file.

How to run

  1. Pick the document.
  2. Click Run.
  3. Read or download the Markdown summary.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-summary.md
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-summary.mdℹ️ — A Markdown summary capturing the document's main points.

Tips

Very long documents may be condensed heavily — for a specific section, point the skill at just that text.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model.This skill needs an LLM. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings.
Summary is thin or generic.The PDF may be a scan with no text layer. Use a text-based document.

GnuCash Import offline · no LLM direct

Part III of the KR Choksey → GnuCash flow. Converts the Part II Bills workbook into importable GnuCash multi-split CSVs — Purchase, SLBM and Sale — using FIFO cost basis and the long-term holding threshold from your .gnucash book and an editable config in Data/settings/. Runs fully offline.

When to use it. Run this last of the three KRChoksey tabs, after Reconcile (Part II). It produces the CSVs you import into GnuCash.

Inputs

  • Part II Bills workbook (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Excel (.xlsx) matching *-KRC-Bills-Recon.xlsx.
  • GnuCash book (.gnucash) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: GnuCash file (.gnucash).

How to run

  1. Pick the Part II Bills workbook from the dropdown.
  2. Select your .gnucash book (closed in GnuCash).
  3. Click Run, then open the output folder.
  4. Import each CSV into GnuCash (File > Import > Import Transactions from CSV).

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-KRC-GnuCash/
  • Purchase.csvℹ️ — One transaction per purchase: Dr the security account (with share quantity), Cr the KR Choksey broker account, for the net/bill amount.
  • SLBM.csvℹ️ — One transaction per SLBM bill: Dr KR Choksey broker, Cr Income from SLBS (net lending proceeds, offered to tax as income).
  • Sale.csvℹ️ — Multi-split per sale: Dr broker (proceeds), Cr security at FIFO cost basis (negative shares), Cr Long/Short Term Capital Gain (a loss appears as a debit).
  • Review.csvℹ️ — Only created when something needs attention — an unmatched security name, or a sale with insufficient FIFO lots.

Tips

Every transaction's Amount column sums to zero, so each imports as a balanced GnuCash transaction. The long-term threshold and account paths come from your book plus the editable config in Data/settings/.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
A Review.csv appears.Open it: either a security name didn't match an account (add/rename it) or a sale lacked enough FIFO lots (check earlier purchases were imported).
Capital gain looks wrong.Confirm the holding-period threshold and account paths in Data/settings/ match your setup.

HDFC offline · no LLM direct

Converts HDFC statements — digital PDF, password-protected PDF, scanned/garbled-text PDF, net-banking XLS/XLSX, or CSV export — into a GnuCash-ready canonical CSV. All four shapes are parsed deterministically (no LLM). A garbled/unusable PDF text layer (custom font encoding) is auto-detected and routed to OCR with Tesseract automatically. Runs offline.

When to use it. Use this to get a clean, consistently-columned CSV from any HDFC statement format. To also map the rows to GnuCash accounts in one step, use the GnuCash Import skill and choose HDFC instead.

Inputs

  • HDFC statement (PDF, XLS, XLSX, or CSV) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: PDF (.pdf), Excel (.xls / .xlsx), or CSV (.csv) — one file.
  • PDF password (only if the statement PDF is password-protected — for HDFC often the Cust ID) (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: The statement's open password — for HDFC this is often the Cust ID.

How to run

  1. Pick your HDFC statement file (XLS/XLSX preferred, PDF and CSV also work).
  2. If the PDF is password-protected, enter its password.
  3. Click Run.
  4. Download the canonical CSV.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-HDFC_canonical.csv
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-HDFC_canonical.csvℹ️ — The statement in canonical columns (date, description, debit/credit, balance) — the same shape every bank skill emits, so it feeds straight into the GnuCash account-mapping pipeline.

Tips

Prefer the net-banking XLS/XLSX export when you have the choice — it skips OCR entirely and is exact. All four input shapes should produce the same rows and balances for the same statement period.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
A scanned or font-garbled PDF produces poor or missing rows.Use the XLS/XLSX/CSV export instead if available. If you only have a PDF, use the bank's original (net-banking download) PDF rather than a derived/re-printed one — printing-to-PDF strips the digital text layer and forces lower-fidelity OCR even when the original would have parsed directly. Ensure Tesseract is available in source mode; OCR fidelity depends on scan quality.
'PDF is password-protected' error.Enter the statement's password in the pdf_password field — for HDFC this is often the account Cust ID.
Columns do not look canonical.Confirm the file is a real HDFC statement; other banks have their own skills.

HSBC offline · no LLM direct

Consolidates a folder of HSBC statement PDFs into one clean, reconciled Excel workbook: it OCRs scanned pages (Tesseract + Poppler) while skipping pages that already have text, extracts the transaction ID, splits out the transaction date, scrubs the description, and adds an Extra Information column plus a Summary sheet.

When to use it. Use this when you have one or more HSBC statement PDFs (savings or Premier) and want a single tidy, reconciled spreadsheet. For account-mapped output into GnuCash, use GnuCash Import and choose HSBC instead.

Inputs

  • Folder containing HSBC statement PDF(s) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: A folder of HSBC PDF statements.

How to run

  1. Put your HSBC PDFs in one folder.
  2. Select that folder.
  3. Click Run — OCR may take a minute on scanned statements.
  4. Download the Excel workbook.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-HSBC.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-HSBC.xlsxℹ️ — A reconciled workbook: one row per transaction with extracted transaction ID, separate transaction date, scrubbed description and an Extra Information column, plus a Summary sheet that ties the totals out.

Tips

Check the Summary sheet first — if its totals reconcile, the extraction is sound. This skill runs fully offline (no LLM, direct mode).

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
OCR output is poor or missing.Ensure Tesseract and Poppler are available (PATH in source mode); use better-quality scans.

ICICI needs LLM endpoint agent

Converts ICICI net-banking statement downloads (the .xls files) into a GnuCash-importable CSV in canonical columns.

When to use it. Use this for the .xls statements ICICI lets you download from net-banking. To also map rows to GnuCash accounts in one step, use GnuCash Import and choose ICICI instead.

Inputs

  • ICICI statement XLS file(s) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Excel 97-2003 (.xls) — one or many.

How to run

  1. Download your statement(s) as .xls from ICICI net-banking.
  2. Select the file(s).
  3. Click Run and download the CSV.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ICICI_GnuCash.csv
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ICICI_GnuCash.csvℹ️ — The statement in canonical date / description / amount / balance columns — the same shape every bank skill emits, ready for the GnuCash account-mapping pipeline.

Tips

Selecting several months at once produces one combined CSV in statement order.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model.ICICI currently uses an LLM step. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings.
Parsing fails or columns are off.Confirm it is the genuine net-banking .xls, not a converted/edited copy.

ITR Workbook offline · no LLM direct

Builds a formula-driven Indian ITR input workbook from a GnuCash "Balance Sheet (eguile)" HTML export (P&L embedded via Retained Earnings). Always runs the full identity/reconciliation check suite (Imbalance == 0, Assets == Equity+Trading+Liabilities+Retained Earnings, every subtotal == sum of its children). Pick the Entity and Assessment Year from the dropdowns (populated from Data/itr/entities.yaml and Data/itr/rules/tax_rules_*.yaml); the selected AY must match the income year inferred from the HTML's own as-of date, or the run hard-fails before any schedule build is attempted. If a matching .gnucash book is also supplied, runs a book<->HTML cross-check per income/expense account. If an entity mapping is also supplied, resolves every leaf to a tag (nearest-ancestor); any unmapped account sets the run status to BLOCKED-FOR-REVIEW and writes a proposed-mappings snippet (with an LLM suggestion per account when an endpoint is configured) ready to paste into the mapping file. If a Form 16 PDF is also supplied, parses its Part B/Annexure-I salary computation, prints the employer TAN, the 115BAC(1A) opt-out election, and the form's own internal consistency checks; an encrypted PDF is decrypted automatically using the selected entity's PAN on file (no need to re-type it). When a mapping is also available, additionally cross-checks 17(1) gross salary against SALARY_GROSS and net tax payable against TAXPAID_TDS_SALARY. If a 26AS workbook is also supplied, ties out reported TDS by quarter and feeds the 234C dividend-quarter buckets. The Regime dropdown overrides the entity's configured regime for this run only (leave blank to use the entity's default for the selected AY). Marginal relief, surcharge caps, age-linked slabs/deductions, and every rate/cap/section referenced in the output are driven entirely from the Rules config for the selected year — nothing is hardcoded.

When to use it. Use once book, mapping, and (optionally) Form 16 / 26AS inputs are ready for an entity and assessment year, to produce the standardized ITR input workbook for review or handoff to a CA. An unmapped account or a selected-AY/HTML-year mismatch blocks the run rather than silently misstating income.

Inputs

  • Balance Sheet (eguile) HTML (required) ℹ️
    accepts: HTML (.html), one file per entity.
  • Entity (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: One of the entities defined in entities.yaml, via dropdown.
  • Assessment Year (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: One of the years found under Data/itr/rules/, via dropdown.
  • Regime (blank = use the entity's configured regime) (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: "new", "old", or blank to use the entity's configured regime.
  • GnuCash book (optional, enables CG lots + dividend quarters) (optional)
    accepts: .gnucash
  • Entity mapping (optional, enables the account-tag resolution + BLOCKED-FOR-REVIEW check) (optional)
    accepts: .yaml
  • Form 16 PDF (optional, fills the Salary sheet; password auto-derived from the selected entity's PAN if encrypted) (optional)
    accepts: .pdf, .PDF
  • 26AS workbook (optional, feeds TDS tie-out + 234C quarters) (optional)
    accepts: .xlsx

How to run

  1. Pick the Balance Sheet (eguile) HTML export.
  2. Select the Entity and Assessment Year from the dropdowns.
  3. Optionally supply the GnuCash book, entity mapping, Form 16 PDF, and/or 26AS workbook.
  4. Click Run to build the workbook and print the verification summary.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ITR.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-ITR.xlsxℹ️ — The full ITR input workbook, or a stub with an ERROR summary if the run was blocked.

Tips

book_file runs the book<->HTML cross-check. mapping_file runs the account-tag resolution; an unmapped account blocks the run and emits a proposed-mappings snippet next to the output file. form16_pdf runs the salary parse + internal consistency checks, plus the Book<->Form16 cross-checks when mapping_file is also supplied; the decryption password is auto-derived from the selected entity's PAN. as26_workbook ties out TDS by quarter and feeds 234C dividend-quarter bucketing. If the Entity/Assessment Year dropdowns look stale after editing entities.yaml or the rules config, use the refresh button beside them.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
"file truncated -- re-export" error.The HTML export is missing its trailing Imbalance Amount row. Re-export from GnuCash.
"ERROR: selected Assessment Year does not match" and only a stub workbook is produced.Pick the Assessment Year that matches the uploaded HTML's as-of date, or upload the HTML for the selected year, then run again.

Import Statement needs LLM endpoint agent

The one-click, end-to-end importer: upload a raw bank statement and your GnuCash book and get back a GnuCash-ready, account-mapped CSV — no intermediate steps. It chains bank extraction, conversion to canonical format, and account mapping for you. HDFC statements are handled fully deterministically (no LLM) across all four input shapes it accepts — normal PDF, password-protected PDF, XLS/XLSX exports, and CSV exports — including automatic OCR fallback if a PDF's text layer is garbled.

When to use it. Use this when your goal is to get transactions into GnuCash. Use the individual bank skills (BoB, HDFC, etc.) instead only when you want the extracted CSV without account mapping.

Inputs

  • Bank (required) ℹ️
    accepts: One of: ICICI, Bank of Baroda, HSBC, HDFC, Kotak, Other Bank (CSV).
  • Statement file(s) — XLS for ICICI; PDF(s) for BoB / HSBC / Kotak; CSV or XLS/XLSX for HDFC / Other (required) ℹ️
    accepts: XLS for ICICI; PDF(s) for BoB / HSBC / Kotak; CSV or XLS/XLSX for HDFC / Other.
  • GnuCash book (.gnucash) — must be closed in GnuCash (required) ℹ️
    accepts: GnuCash file (.gnucash).
  • PDF password (HDFC only — if the statement PDF is password-protected, for HDFC often the Cust ID) (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: The statement's open password — for HDFC this is often the Cust ID.

How to run

  1. Choose the bank.
  2. Upload the matching statement file(s).
  3. Select your .gnucash book (closed in GnuCash).
  4. Click Run, then import the resulting CSV via File > Import in GnuCash.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-GnuCash_import_ready.csv
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-GnuCash_import_ready.csvℹ️ — The statement converted to canonical columns AND mapped to your GnuCash accounts (based on your book's history), ready to import directly — no manual categorising.

Tips

Account mapping learns from your existing GnuCash history, so the more you have categorised before, the better the auto-mapping. Review a few rows after import the first time. For HDFC, prefer the plain PDF, XLS/XLSX, or CSV export over a password-protected PDF when you have the choice — extraction is equally deterministic either way, but if HDFC ever hands you a PDF whose text layer is garbled (rare, seen on some re-downloaded statements), the skill auto-detects it and falls back to OCR, which is typically ~90%+ accurate rather than exact; re-check the running/closing balance in that case.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model.Mapping (and 'Other Bank' normalisation) uses an LLM. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings.
Extraction fails immediately.The file type probably doesn't match the chosen bank (e.g. a PDF selected for ICICI). Re-check both.
Some rows mapped to the wrong account.Fix them in GnuCash after import; future runs learn from the correction.

Intercompany Matrix offline · no LLM direct

An optional roll-up over the pairwise Intercompany Reco: it reconciles every unordered pair among the books you select and rolls them into one workbook -- a colour-coded owner-by-owner difference grid, a per-pair summary, and one consolidated exceptions list. Same deterministic engine, read-only on your books.

When to use it. Run at year-end to see, at a glance, which family members' books agree and which do not -- instead of running the two-book reconciliation for every combination by hand.

Inputs

  • GnuCash books (.gnucash) -- pick 2 or more; all must be closed in GnuCash (required) ℹ️
    accepts: GnuCash files (.gnucash).
  • Reconciliation period (Indian FY = 1 Apr to 31 Mar) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: A FY, a calendar year, Auto, or a custom date range.
  • Custom start date (YYYY-MM-DD) -- only for 'Custom date range' (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: YYYY-MM-DD.
  • Custom end date (YYYY-MM-DD) -- only for 'Custom date range' (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: YYYY-MM-DD.
  • Date tolerance in days for matching in-transit items (default 7) (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: A whole number of days. Default 7.

How to run

  1. Select all the .gnucash books to cross-reconcile (closed in GnuCash).
  2. Choose the period (or leave on Auto).
  3. Click Run and download the matrix workbook.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Intercompany-Matrix.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Intercompany-Matrix.xlsxℹ️ — Matrix sheet (owner-by-owner difference grid, green ties / red out of balance / grey n/a), Pairs sheet (balances and counts per pair), and All Exceptions (every unmatched item across all pairs with the best probable posting found in the other book).

Tips

Start from the Matrix grid: any red cell is a pair to open. The All Exceptions sheet is the single actionable to-do list across the whole family.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
A pair shows 'n/a'.Those two books do not each keep an account named for the other. That is normal for people who do not transact.
A pair is red (out of balance) with a large difference.Open it in the two-book Intercompany Reco for full Matched / Exceptions detail, then fix the mis-postings it lists.

Intercompany Reco offline · no LLM direct

Reconciles the contra accounts two people keep for each other across their two GnuCash books. It matches the current financial year's movements, carries the opening balance forward, and for every unmatched item searches the other book for a probable mis-posting -- then writes an Excel reconciliation statement. Fully offline and deterministic (no LLM); read-only on your books.

When to use it. Run at year-end (or before filing) to confirm that what one person's book shows as due to/from the other agrees with the other person's book -- and to catch entries one side recorded that the other missed or mis-posted.

Inputs

  • First person's GnuCash book (.gnucash) -- must be closed in GnuCash (required) ℹ️
    accepts: GnuCash file (.gnucash).
  • Second person's GnuCash book (.gnucash) -- must be closed in GnuCash (required) ℹ️
    accepts: GnuCash file (.gnucash).
  • Reconciliation period (Indian FY = 1 Apr to 31 Mar) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: A FY, a calendar year, Auto, or a custom date range.
  • Custom start date (YYYY-MM-DD) -- only for 'Custom date range' (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: YYYY-MM-DD.
  • Custom end date (YYYY-MM-DD) -- only for 'Custom date range' (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: YYYY-MM-DD.
  • Date tolerance in days for matching in-transit items (default 7) (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: A whole number of days. Default 7.

How to run

  1. Pick both people's .gnucash books (closed in GnuCash).
  2. Choose the reconciliation period (or leave on Auto).
  3. Optionally adjust the date tolerance.
  4. Click Run and download the reconciliation workbook.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Intercompany-Recon.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Intercompany-Recon.xlsxℹ️ — The reconciliation statement: a Summary (opening, movements, closing, and the balance difference), a Matched sheet pairing both books' entries, and an Exceptions sheet per side listing unmatched items with probable mis-postings found in the other book.

Tips

If the balance ties but exceptions remain, those items usually net to zero (a give-and-take one side recorded and the other did not) -- still worth fixing. Descriptions mentioning 'HUF' against an individual's contra account signal an entry that belongs in the HUF book instead.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
No contra account found.The other person's name must appear in an account name in each book. Rename the account to include their name, then re-run.
An item shows 'No candidate found in the other book'.The other person genuinely never recorded it, OR it belongs to a related entity (e.g. an HUF) whose book was not the one you loaded.
Too many unmatched items that clearly correspond.Increase the date tolerance -- the payments are landing in the other book more than a week apart.

Journal needs LLM endpoint agent

Builds GnuCash TDS journal entries from a 26AS workbook plus your GnuCash book. It reads Part I of the workbook, matches each deductor to one of your accounts (deterministic fuzzy match first, LLM fallback for the hard cases), and writes a balanced journal CSV ready to import.

When to use it. Use this after "26AS Convert", when you want the TDS recorded in GnuCash as journal entries instead of typing each one by hand.

Inputs

  • 26AS workbook (Convert output) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Excel (.xlsx) matching *-26AS.xlsx.
  • GnuCash file (required) ℹ️
    accepts: GnuCash file (.gnucash).

How to run

  1. Pick the 26AS workbook from the dropdown of prior outputs.
  2. Select your .gnucash book (make sure GnuCash has it closed).
  3. Click Run; review any deductors flagged as unmatched.
  4. Import the CSV into GnuCash: File > Import > Import Transactions from CSV.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-tds-journals.csv
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-tds-journals.csvℹ️ — One balanced journal transaction per TDS entry (every transaction's amounts sum to zero), debiting the TDS receivable / asset account and crediting the income account, ready for GnuCash CSV import.

Tips

Entries whose deductor could not be matched to an account are flagged so you can fix the account name in your book (or add the missing account) and re-run.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model.This skill uses an LLM for fallback matching. Start Ollama (or set an endpoint in Settings).
A deductor was assigned the wrong account.Rename/add the account in GnuCash so its name is closer to the deductor, then re-run.

KRChoksey offline · no LLM direct

Part I of the KR Choksey → GnuCash flow. Simplifies the annual KR Choksey broker ledger-statement PDF into a clean, tied-out "Simplified Ledger" Excel workbook. Runs fully offline (deterministic, no LLM).

When to use it. Run this first of the three KRChoksey sub-tabs. Its workbook is the input to "Reconcile" (Part II), which in turn feeds "GnuCash Import" (Part III).

Inputs

  • KR Choksey Ledger Statement PDF (required) ℹ️
    accepts: PDF (.pdf) — the ledger statement.
  • PDF password (account holder's PAN) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Text — the account holder's PAN.

How to run

  1. Select the KR Choksey ledger statement PDF.
  2. Enter the PDF password (the account holder's PAN).
  3. Click Run and download the Simplified Ledger workbook.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Ledger.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Ledger.xlsxℹ️ — The Simplified Ledger: the annual statement re-laid as a clean, tied-out workbook. This is the file you pick in the Reconcile (Part II) tab.

Tips

Run the three KRChoksey tabs in order — Ledger, then Reconcile, then GnuCash Import. Each later tab picks this output from a dropdown of prior runs.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run fails opening the PDF.Check the password is the correct PAN. Ensure qpdf is available in source mode.
Rows look incomplete.Confirm you selected the full annual ledger statement, not a single contract note.

Kotak offline · no LLM direct

Turns a Kotak Mahindra Bank savings-account transaction-statement PDF into a flat CSV — one row per transaction. Runs fully offline (no LLM, no internet) and reads the statement's ruled/bordered table structure directly, so a table that overflows from one page to the next (without repeating the column header) is still captured correctly, and the trailing abbreviation-legend page is excluded rather than mistaken for transaction rows.

When to use it. Use this when you have a Kotak savings-account statement as a PDF and want it as a tidy CSV you can open in Excel, reconcile, or feed into the GnuCash Import pipeline (choose "Kotak" there to do extraction + account mapping in one step instead). This skill is extraction only — it does not categorise or assign accounts.

Inputs

  • Kotak statement PDF (required) ℹ️
    accepts: PDF (.pdf) — one statement per run.

How to run

  1. Click the input and pick a Kotak statement PDF.
  2. Click Run — it processes offline in a few seconds.
  3. Download the CSV, or open it from the outputs folder (see below).

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Kotak.csv
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Kotak.csvℹ️ — One row per transaction with columns: #, Date, Description, Chq/Ref. No., Withdrawal, Deposit, Balance. Rows are in statement order; the "Opening Balance" front-matter row and the trailing abbreviation-legend page are both excluded from the CSV.

Tips

The running balance carries straight from the statement — if the final Balance matches your passbook, the extraction tied out. Sweep-in/sweep-out transfers to a linked Fixed Deposit are kept as real transactions, not filtered out. Columns are kept verbatim from the statement so amounts are not rounded or reformatted.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
The output CSV is empty or has very few rows.The PDF is probably a scanned image, not a text PDF. Re-download the original statement from Kotak net-banking, or run it through an OCR step first. This skill does not OCR.
Run fails saying the PDF could not be opened.The PDF is password-protected. Unlock it (open in a viewer, print/save as a new unprotected PDF) and try again.
Closing balance in the CSV does not match my passbook.Check the statement's last page — the abbreviation legend on that page is intentionally excluded, but if any real transaction rows sit above it, make sure the whole document (all pages) was included when you selected the PDF.

MSG / Email Parser offline · no LLM direct

Parses an Outlook .msg or standard .eml email into structured JSON: sender, date, subject, body text and the list of attachments. Pure deterministic extraction — no LLM, fully offline.

When to use it. Use this when you need an email's fields as data (for a script, a record, or further processing) rather than reading it in a mail client.

Inputs

  • Email file (.msg or .eml) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Outlook .msg or RFC-822 .eml — one file.

How to run

  1. Select the .msg or .eml file.
  2. Click Run.
  3. Download the JSON.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-email-parsed.json
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-email-parsed.jsonℹ️ — Structured JSON with sender, date, subject, body text and an attachments list — easy to read into any script or tool.

Tips

Attachments are listed (names), so you can see what was enclosed without opening the mail.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Body text looks empty.Some HTML-only emails store the body differently; check the JSON fields — the text may be under the HTML body.

Parser Generator needs LLM endpoint agent

A developer-time tool that creates or repairs the project's embedded deterministic parsers (src/agents/skill_<X>/scripts/parse_<format>.py). It is template-based: the model may only fill format-specific blanks (column ranges, regexes, row-boundary and skip rules); the balance oracle and the 0/1/2 exit contract are off-limits. Every result must pass the quality gate (validate -> tie-out exit 0) before you commit it.

When to use it. Use "Fix a failing parser" when a statement format changed and a parser's tie-out fails (the oracle exits 2). Use "Create a new parser" to scaffold one for a new format from the shared template. This is for maintainers, not end-of-day statement processing. See docs/Parser-Generator-Guide.md.

Inputs

  • Task (required) ℹ️
    accepts: One of: Fix a failing parser, Create a new parser.
  • Parser (pick a known parser to Fix, or type a new path to Create) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: A known parser (pick from the list) or a new parse_<format>.py path.
  • Tie-out args (Fix only: the parser's CLI args, e.g. 'statement.pdf PASSWORD out.xlsx') (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: Text, e.g. 'statement.pdf PASSWORD out.xlsx'. Optional.
  • Notes (Fix: the symptom / expected closing balance; Create: the format / FORMAT_NAME) (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: Text. Fix: the symptom / expected closing balance. Create: the format / FORMAT_NAME. Optional.

How to run

  1. Choose Fix or Create.
  2. Select the parser (or type the new path).
  3. For Fix, provide the tie-out args and the symptom / expected balance in Notes.
  4. Click Run; review the report, and only commit once tie-out exits 0.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-parser-gen/
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-parser-gen/ (a folder)ℹ️ — A report folder with the proposed parser change and the validate / tie-out results. Inspect it before committing the parser.

Tips

The runtime stays deterministic — this tool only edits the format-specific blanks of a parser; the balance oracle and exit contract are never touched.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model.This dev tool uses an LLM. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings.
Tie-out still exits non-zero.Add the expected closing balance to Notes and re-run; if it persists, the format may need a manual template tweak.

Reconcile offline · no LLM direct

Part II of the KR Choksey → GnuCash flow. Matches the contract notes (SLBM memos and trade notes) against the Part I Simplified Ledger and produces a "Bills" workbook. Runs fully offline (deterministic, no LLM).

When to use it. Run this second, after "KRChoksey" (Part I). Its Bills workbook is the input to "GnuCash Import" (Part III).

Inputs

  • Part I Simplified Ledger workbook (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Excel (.xlsx) matching *-KRC-Ledger.xlsx.
  • Contract Notes (SLBM memos + trade notes) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: PDF (.pdf) — one or many contract notes.
  • PDF password (account holder's PAN) (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Text — the account holder's PAN.

How to run

  1. Pick the Part I Simplified Ledger from the dropdown.
  2. Select all the contract-note PDFs.
  3. Enter the PDF password (PAN).
  4. Click Run and download the Bills workbook.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Bills-Recon.xlsx
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-KRC-Bills-Recon.xlsxℹ️ — The reconciled Bills workbook tying contract notes to the ledger. This is the file you pick in the GnuCash Import (Part III) tab.

Tips

A complete set of contract notes is what makes Part II tie out — missing notes show up as unmatched bills.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Some bills are unmatched.A contract note is probably missing for the period. Add it and re-run.
PDF won't open.Check the PAN password and that qpdf is available in source mode.

Text Translator needs LLM endpoint direct

Translates text between languages using your configured LLM, and saves the translation as a text file.

When to use it. Use this for quick text translation. Pick 'auto' as the source language to let the model detect it.

Inputs

  • Text to translate (required) ℹ️
    accepts: Free text.
  • Source language (optional) ℹ️
    accepts: auto, English, Hindi, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Chinese, Arabic, Portuguese, Russian.
  • Target language (required) ℹ️
    accepts: English, Hindi, Spanish, French, German, Japanese, Chinese, Arabic, Portuguese, Russian.

How to run

  1. Paste the text.
  2. Set source (or 'auto') and target language.
  3. Click Run and download the translation.

Output

Written to Data/outputs/:

Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-translation.txt
  • YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-translation.txtℹ️ — The translated text in the target language.

Tips

Translation quality depends on the model you have connected — a larger model generally handles nuance and less-common languages better.

Troubleshooting

If…Then…
Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model.This skill needs an LLM. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings.
Output mixes languages or is off.Set the source language explicitly instead of 'auto', or try a larger model.
Generated from skill manifests · 21 UI skills, 6 internal steps.
